description: Send instant notifications, status updates, or alerts to a designated Slack channel using webhooks.

parameters.name: channel_id
parameters.description: Target Slack channel name or identifier (e.g., #engineering, C12345)

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: The actual text content to publish to the channel.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Custom display name for the bot sender.

parameters.name: webhook_url
parameters.description: Optional direct webhook endpoint to bypass default routing.

examples.description: Notify developers about successful build
Send a critical system warning to ops

return_type.description: Confirmation detailing request status and timestamp of delivery.

description: Create a compressed backup archive of the specified PostgreSQL or MySQL database and upload it to secure storage.

parameters.name: db_name
parameters.description: Name of the target database schema.

parameters.name: storage_bucket
parameters.description: S3 or Cloud Storage bucket name to save the backup zip.

parameters.name: compression_level
parameters.description: Level of compression (1-9), defaults to 6.

examples.description: Create user database backup
Create highly compressed archive of inventory database

return_type.description: Object specifying backup filename, file size, bucket location, and SHA-256 hash.

description: Execute a read-only SQL SELECT query against the active database cluster to fetch data records.

parameters.name: sql_query
parameters.description: The SQL query string to run. Must be read-only (SELECT).

parameters.name: db_cluster
parameters.description: Target cluster name (primary, replica-us, replica-eu).

parameters.name: row_limit
parameters.description: Max number of rows to return, defaults to 100.

examples.usage: query_database(sql_query='SELECT * FROM users WHERE status = "active" LIMIT 10')
examples.description: Query active user records
Count total orders in EU replica

return_type.description: Array of JSON objects matching rows returned by the database server.

description: Deploy a specified Docker image tag to the cloud environment (ECS, Kubernetes, or App Runner).

parameters.name: image_uri
parameters.description: Docker image registry path (e.g. gcr.io/my-project/api:v2.1.0)

parameters.name: environment
parameters.description: Target deployment tier (staging, production, sandbox)

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: Application listening port inside container.

parameters.name: replicas
parameters.description: Target number of container instances, defaults to 2.

examples.description: Deploy nginx to staging
Deploy custom API to production with 5 instances

return_type.description: Deployment status, endpoint URLs, and deployment revision ID.

description: Send HTML/Text email reports, alerts, or newsletters through secure SMTP or SendGrid provider.

parameters.name: recipient
parameters.description: Target email address of the receiver.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: Subject line of the email.

parameters.name: body_content
parameters.description: Text or HTML body content of the message.

parameters.name: attachments
parameters.description: List of file paths or S3 URIs to attach.

examples.usage: send_email_report(recipient='ceo@company.com', subject='Q2 Performance Report', body_content='Please find Q2 metrics attached')
examples.description: Email performance report to management

examples.usage: send_email_report(recipient='devs@company.com', subject='Alert: System Outage', body_content='API is down in prod')
examples.description: Send high-priority email alert

return_type.description: Delivery message ID and transmission status code.

description: Query and retrieve recent application logs from centralized log management systems (CloudWatch, ELK, Datadog) based on filters.

parameters.name: service_name
parameters.description: Name of the microservice (e.g. auth-service, gateway).

parameters.name: log_level
parameters.description: Log level filter (ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG).

parameters.name: duration_minutes
parameters.description: Time window in minutes to look back, defaults to 15.

parameters.name: search_pattern
parameters.description: Keyword or regex to search inside logs.

examples.description: Get recent errors in payment service
Search gateway logs for timeouts

return_type.description: Array of log entries with timestamp, log level, and raw log message.

description: Scale the replication count for a specific Kubernetes Deployment resource.

parameters.name: deployment_name
parameters.description: Name of the target deployment resource.

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: Kubernetes namespace (default, production, staging).

parameters.name: replicas
parameters.description: The desired number of running replicas.

examples.description: Scale auth deployment replicas to 4
Scale production front-end to 10 instances

return_type.description: Result summary with target replicas, active replicas, and namespace context.

description: List files, directories, and metadata stored inside an Amazon Web Services S3 bucket.

parameters.name: bucket_name
parameters.description: The name of the target AWS S3 bucket.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: Directory path prefix to filter objects.

parameters.name: max_keys
parameters.description: Max keys to list in a single batch, defaults to 1000.

examples.description: List reports from the 2026 directory
List first 10 items in media bucket

return_type.description: List of files with keys, size in bytes, and last modified dates.

description: Fetch real-time host hardware metrics (CPU load, memory load, disk IO) from telemetry targets (Prometheus).

parameters.name: node_id
parameters.description: Target host machine name or IP address.

parameters.name: metric_type
parameters.description: Metric class to pull (cpu, memory, disk, network), defaults to cpu.

parameters.name: interval
parameters.description: Time interval step (e.g. 5m, 1h).

examples.description: Retrieve memory usage from prod-db-01
Retrieve hourly CPU usage from worker

return_type.description: Time series data containing timestamps and float values.

description: Create a Git Pull Request or Merge Request automatically to merge code changes from development branch to release branch.

parameters.name: repository
parameters.description: Repository namespace/name (e.g., github/workspace/api-service).

parameters.name: source_branch
parameters.description: The feature branch containing commits.

parameters.name: target_branch
parameters.description: The branch to merge into (usually main or develop).

parameters.name: pr_title
parameters.description: Title of the Pull Request.

parameters.name: pr_description
parameters.description: Body description/release notes for reviewer.

examples.description: Open pull request for OAuth branch

return_type.description: Pull request details including PR URL, state, ID, and merge status.

description: Send real-time alerts, status updates, or notifications to a designated Discord channel using official Webhook APIs.

parameters.name: webhook_url
parameters.description: The unique Discord webhook endpoint URL.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The message text content (supports Discord markdown formatting).

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Overrides the webhook bot's default username.

parameters.name: avatar_url
parameters.description: Overrides the webhook bot's default profile image link.

examples.description: Send failed backup alert to discord

examples.usage: send_discord_message(webhook_url='https://discord.com/api/webhooks/...', content='New release deployed to staging.', username='Staging Bot')
examples.description: Send release success confirmation

return_type.description: Confirmation details including HTTP response code and transmission status.

description: Compile and execute a binary program directly from raw hex instruction bytes or machine language to run operations with maximum compression and speed, cutting down text representation size.

parameters.name: hex_instructions
parameters.description: Raw hexadecimal encoded string of compile target/machine instructions.

parameters.name: architecture
parameters.description: Target runtime architecture (x86_64, arm64, wasm).

parameters.name: memory_limit_mb
parameters.description: Max memory allocation limit in MB, defaults to 64.

examples.description: Execute a raw x86_64 'Hello World' hex instruction assembly

return_type.description: Execution report specifying exit code, stdout string, stderr string, execution time, and CPU cycle consumption.

description: Spawn and run an asynchronous background process or system script autonomously. The process runs decoupled from the active agent thread, allowing continuous execution, telemetry collection, or task queue monitoring without blocking the session or waiting for interactive user response.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The binary, script, or command to execute in the background.

parameters.name: working_dir
parameters.description: Working directory where the process is spawned.

parameters.name: log_file
parameters.description: File path to redirect standard output and stderr streams to capture logs.

parameters.name: auto_restart
parameters.description: Restart the process automatically if it crashes or terminates with a non-zero exit code, defaults to false.

examples.description: Spawn worker.go daemon with logging and auto-restart policy

return_type.description: Details containing the process ID (PID), log target, status (running), and timestamp.

description: Search for specific string patterns or regular expressions inside project source code files to locate class definitions, function calls, or configuration keys.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: The text pattern or regular expression to search for.

parameters.name: file_extensions
parameters.description: Filter files to search by extensions (e.g. ['.go', '.json']).

examples.description: Locate NewSearchEngine instantiation in Go source files

return_type.description: Matches found, indicating file path, line numbers, and matching content line snippet.

description: Compile and execute the Go package test suite automatically, generating code coverage metrics and reporting test failures.

parameters.name: package_path
parameters.description: Specific Go package folder to test, defaults to all packages ('./...').

parameters.name: run_pattern
parameters.description: Specific test function name pattern to run (e.g. TestSearchEngine).

parameters.name: enable_coverage
parameters.description: Generate a test coverage profile report, defaults to false.

examples.description: Run the configuration loader unit test

return_type.description: Test results summary detailing exit code, test stdout/stderr report, elapsed time, and coverage percent.

description: Compile the Go project files to verify syntax, imports, and cross-package dependencies without generating a final binary executable.

parameters.name: target_os
parameters.description: Target OS architecture to compile for (linux, darwin, windows).

parameters.name: verbose
parameters.description: Enable verbose compilation output logging, defaults to false.

examples.description: Verify compilation compatibility on Linux OS

return_type.description: Report containing status (success/failed), error details if compilation failed, and warning outputs.

description: Query the Git version control system to identify modified files, untracked files, or uncommitted changes in the active working directory.

parameters.name: include_diff
parameters.description: Include detailed git diff lines in the returned results, defaults to false.

examples.description: Check git repository status including modified code lines diff

return_type.description: Summary containing current branch, modified files list, untracked files list, and optional diff string.

description: Manage the tools stored in the SurrealDB vector database registry. Supports listing all tools, viewing details, creating/registering a tool, and deleting a tool.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The management action to perform: 'list', 'view', 'create', 'delete'.

parameters.name: tool_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the tool (required for 'view', 'create', 'delete').

parameters.name: definition_path
parameters.description: Path to a JSON file containing the tool definition schema (required for 'create').

examples.description: List all active tools registered in the database
Remove an obsolete tool from the registry

return_type.description: Details showing list of tools, schema content, or operation success status.

description: Manage the embedded SurrealDB daemon process lifecycle. Supports starting the daemon, stopping the daemon, checking running status, and viewing daemon console output logs.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The database lifecycle action to perform: 'start', 'stop', 'status', 'logs'.

parameters.name: log_lines
parameters.description: Number of console log lines to display (only applicable for 'logs' action), defaults to 15.

examples.description: Start the SurrealDB background process
Display process ID (PID) and status of SurrealDB daemon

return_type.description: Status report detailing process state, PID, or logs text.

description: Run code formatters and static analysis linters (like gofmt, pylint, eslint, black) on source files to verify code style and detect syntax warnings.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Path to the source code file to format or lint.

parameters.name: auto_fix
parameters.description: Automatically apply formatting and lint corrections if supported by the tool.

examples.description: Format and fix style errors in main.go

return_type.description: Lint report listing style issues, syntax errors, and status of auto-fixes.

description: Control system service states (start, stop, restart, enable, disable, status) on the host machine using systemd controls.

parameters.name: service_name
parameters.description: Name of the target service (e.g., nginx, docker, ssh).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Service lifecycle action to perform: 'start', 'stop', 'restart', 'status'.

examples.description: Restart nginx web server service
Check status of docker daemon

return_type.description: Execution result including command exit code and stdout/stderr output from systemctl.

description: Audit security and authentication logs (e.g. /var/log/auth.log, /var/log/secure) to discover failed logins, root privilege escalations, or SSH access events.

parameters.name: max_entries
parameters.description: Maximum number of recent log lines or events to analyze, defaults to 100.

parameters.name: grep_filter
parameters.description: Optional keyword pattern filter (e.g. 'failed', 'sudo', 'root').

examples.description: Audit security logs for failed login attempts

return_type.description: List of parsed log audit events containing timestamp, host, process, and warning description.

description: Perform a network port scan against a specified target IP or host to identify open ports, active services, and potential vulnerabilities.

parameters.name: target_host
parameters.description: Domain name or IP address to scan (e.g. localhost, 192.168.1.1).

parameters.name: port_range
parameters.description: Range of ports to scan (e.g., '22-80', '1-1024', or '80,443'), defaults to '1-1024'.

parameters.name: scan_type
parameters.description: Scan method to employ: 'TCP-SYN', 'TCP-Connect', defaults to 'TCP-Connect'.

examples.description: Scan ports 80, 443, and 8000 on localhost

return_type.description: List of open ports found, including port number, protocol, state (open/closed), and guessed service name.

description: Scans directories and source code files for sensitive hardcoded secrets, such as private keys, API tokens, passwords, and access credentials.

parameters.name: target_directory
parameters.description: Absolute or relative directory path to search.

parameters.name: exclude_patterns
parameters.description: List of file glob patterns to skip (e.g. ['.git', 'node_modules']).

examples.description: Scan project folder for credentials

return_type.description: List of potential credential leaks found, including file name, line number, pattern matched, and obfuscated secret snippet.

description: Scan application dependency lockfiles (such as go.sum, package-lock.json, requirements.txt) against known vulnerability databases (e.g. OSV, CVE) to detect outdated or insecure libraries.

parameters.name: project_directory
parameters.description: Path to the project directory containing dependency files.

parameters.name: ignore_dev
parameters.description: Skip scanning development-only dependencies, defaults to false.

examples.description: Scan project production dependencies for vulnerabilities

return_type.description: List of insecure packages found, including package name, installed version, fixed version, CVE ID, and severity level.

description: Analyze the TLS/SSL configuration, supported cipher suites, protocol versions (e.g. TLS 1.0, 1.3), and certificate validity of a target web endpoint to find insecure encryption settings.

parameters.name: target_url
parameters.description: Target hostname or endpoint address to audit (e.g. secure.example.com).

examples.description: Audit SSL/TLS cipher suites and certificate expiration on example.com

return_type.description: TLS audit report detailing supported protocol versions, weak ciphers, and certificate status.

description: Save a text fact, configuration preference, architectural decision, or task log in the persistent agent memory database. This allows persisting context across CLI sessions and agent runs.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The actual factual statement, preference description, or execution log details to save.

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: The isolated session namespace/context (e.g., project name, active git branch, or 'global' for cross-session preferences).

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Type of memory: 'preference' (guidelines), 'episodic' (general facts), or 'task_log' (execution logs). Defaults to 'episodic'.

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: Optional background task identifier to associate with log entries.

examples.usage: rtk ./sts-surreal -memory-add "User prefers Docker deployments on port 8080" -session "web-app" -category "preference"
examples.description: Save a project-scoped deployment preference

examples.usage: rtk ./sts-surreal -memory-add "Build failed: compiler error in main.go:45" -session "web-app" -category "task_log" -task-id "task_101"
examples.description: Record an execution log entry for a specific task

return_type.description: Confirmation message containing the saved record ID.

description: Retrieve relevant context, decisions, and configuration preferences from the agent memory database using semantic (KNN) vector similarity. Returns both session-specific and global matches.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query or task context to find matching memories for.

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: The active session namespace/context (e.g., project name, git branch). Will automatically include global memories.

examples.description: Retrieve configuration preferences related to deployment port

return_type.description: List of matching memory entries with their ID, category, content, and timestamp.

description: List episodic context entries, preferences, or task progress logs in chronological (time-series) order for a specific session namespace.

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: The target session namespace to list.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional filter to restrict results to a specific category (e.g., 'task_log', 'preference').

examples.description: View the execution log history for the web-app session

return_type.description: Chronological list of memory entries matching the filters.

description: Purge all memory entries associated with a specific session namespace to ensure session isolation and start with a clean slate. Does not delete global memories.

parameters.name: session
parameters.description: The session namespace to clear.

examples.description: Wipe all memories for the web-app session

return_type.description: Confirmation status indicating successful cleanup.

description: Optimize and compress wordy text prompts, application log dumps, stack traces, or large file contents to extract the core instruction and remove redundant chunks before routing to the main LLM. This significantly reduces token usage.

parameters.name: text_content
parameters.description: The raw wordy text, console output, stack trace, or file contents to optimize.

examples.usage: rtk ./sts-surreal -optimize-text "[ERROR] 2026-06-18T14:55:14 ... compiler error in main.go:45 ... (repeated 50 times)"
examples.description: Optimize and compress a redundant stack trace error log dump

return_type.description: A formatted optimized text block containing the extracted core intent and relevant deduplicated chunks.
